quotes.text: There's a version of me that retires at twenty-eight and opens a restaurant in Biarritz and I think about that guy sometimes. He looks very relaxed.
quotes.speaker: Zinedine Zidane
quotes.context: France Football magazine long-form career retrospective interview, published December 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Zidane has spoken warmly about the French Basque coast and food, and his interviews have this gentle, slightly melancholic humor about roads not taken. The tell is the specific age — he'd more likely leave it vague.

quotes.text: Baseball has been good to me since I quit trying to play it.
quotes.speaker: Whitey Herzog
quotes.context: The Hall of Fame manager reflecting on his so-so playing career versus his success as a skipper.

source.title: ESPN Page 2 — My 5 top quotes by Hall of Famer Whitey Herzog

quotes.text: Has anyone else 'completed football' like this?
quotes.speaker: Owen Bagnall
quotes.context: The Knowledge column, asking about Rodri completing every major honour

source.title: Sports — World Cup, Ballon d’Or, Champions League, Euros … has Rodri completed football?

quotes.text: Pressure is a privilege and champions adjust.
quotes.speaker: Billie Jean King
quotes.context: The line she says spontaneously came out of her mouth while motivating Lindsay Davenport as U.S. Fed Cup captain, recounted at Tennis.com.

source.title: Tennis.com — Billie Jean King shares origins of 'Pressure is a privilege'

quotes.text: A fast bowler who tells you he never wants to hurt the batsman is lying to you, or he's in the wrong job. You want to see a flicker. You're not trying to hospitalise anyone, but you need that flicker.
quotes.speaker: Shane Warne
quotes.context: Sky Sports Cricket Podcast, recorded in Melbourne during Australia's summer Test series, January 2020
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Warne was famous for frank, slightly provocative takes on cricket's competitive psychology, and this is precisely the kind of thing that would spark a news cycle the next day. The tell is Warne was a spinner who'd typically couch fast-bowling psychology from the outside, not with this ownership.

quotes.text: My hamstring told me it was done before my brain did. I was mid-stride and I remember thinking, that's not fatigue, that's goodbye. I finished the rep anyway. Stupid, probably.
quotes.speaker: Usain Bolt
quotes.context: BBC Sport documentary 'Bolt', filmed in Kingston ahead of the 2017 World Athletics Championships in London
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bolt's real interviews have exactly this mix of self-deprecating humor and casual confidence about his body. The tell is that his actual injury talk from 2017 is more emotional and less wry.
